quick_facts.label: What this node covers
quick_facts.value: Agniveer is an umbrella recruitment route under the Agnipath scheme, not one identical examination: the Army uses category-based Online CEE followed by a recruitment rally; Navy SSR uses INET and subsequent selection stages; and the Air Force publishes intake-specific Agniveervayu selection notices.

quick_facts.label: Education routes represented
quick_facts.value: Eligibility is category-specific. For example, the 2026 Army notice permits Class 10 entry for Agniveer General Duty and sets PCM/English or specified ITI/vocational routes for Technical; the Navy SSR notice specifies 10+2 Mathematics and Physics, or listed engineering-diploma/vocational alternatives.

quick_facts.label: Engagement term
quick_facts.value: The Army and Navy notices describe Agniveer enrolment as a four-year engagement. Further regular-cadre enrolment is not guaranteed; the cited notices state that up to 25% of a specific batch may be enrolled, subject to service requirements, policy, and performance.

quick_facts.label: Current intake example
quick_facts.value: A Government of Odisha posting for IAF Agniveervayu Intake 02/2027 lists online registration from June 23, 2026 through July 26, 2026. Dates and availability are intake-specific and must be confirmed on the service recruitment portal before applying.

quick_facts.label: Army fee context
quick_facts.value: For the 2026 Army recruitment notice, the stated online-examination fee was ₹250 per application category, plus applicable bank charges. This is dated recruitment-notice context, not a promise for future intakes.

sections.heading: Purpose and route structure
paragraphs: Agniveer recruitment is the entry route for eligible youth to serve below commissioned-officer level in the Indian Army, Indian Navy, or Indian Air Force under the Agnipath framework. It should be treated as a family of service- and category-specific recruitments rather than as a single national examination with one common syllabus, qualification threshold, or calendar.

For the Army, the 2026 notice describes a two-phase process: an Online Common Entrance Examination (CEE) at allotted computer-based centres, followed by an Army Recruiting Office recruitment rally. The Navy SSR notice uses Stage I INET followed by Stage II selection; IAF Agniveervayu recruitment is published by intake, with its own registration and selection notice.
bullets: Choose the service and entry category first; only then compare education, age, physical, medical, domicile, and document requirements.
Use only the official recruitment portal and the notification for the relevant intake or recruiting year; older notices are useful for orientation but do not replace the current one.

sections.heading: Eligibility: match the exact service and category
paragraphs: Do not infer eligibility from the umbrella term “Agniveer.” The Army’s 2026 notification, for example, lists Class 10 with specified marks for Agniveer General Duty, while Agniveer Technical requires a science/PCM-and-English pathway or certain alternative ITI/vocational qualifications. The Navy SSR notice requires 10+2 with Mathematics and Physics and 50% aggregate marks, or specified engineering-diploma or vocational-course alternatives with 50% aggregate marks.

Age bands, marital-status conditions, sex/category availability, physical standards, medical standards, permitted relaxations, and required certificates are all notification-specific. The cited Army and Navy notices require unmarried candidates for the relevant entries and require an undertaking not to marry during the four-year engagement; candidates should not generalize a regional or one-intake notice to another service or intake.
bullets: Class 10 is relevant to some Army categories, especially General Duty; it is not a universal qualification for every Agniveer role.
PCM, ITI, diploma, and vocational pathways can be relevant to technical entries, but the acceptable subjects, marks, NSQF level, and trades must be checked against the current notification.
Check date-of-birth cut-offs rather than relying only on a stated age range, because notices commonly publish exact eligible birth-date windows.
Verify physical measurements, fitness standards, medical requirements, and documentary proofs before applying.

sections.heading: Selection format
paragraphs: Army Agniveer selection in the cited 2026 notice has two phases. Phase I is a computer-based, objective multiple-choice CEE. Depending on category, it has either 50 questions in one hour or 100 questions in two hours; the notice also specifies a typing test during CEE for Agniveer Clerk/Store Keeper Technical candidates. Candidates are shortlisted from the CEE result for Phase II, the recruitment rally.

The cited Army recruitment-rally update identifies the subsequent procedures for shortlisted candidates as Physical Fitness Test, Physical Measurement Test, physical-efficiency events, medical examination, and other rally procedures. For Navy SSR, the official notice identifies Stage I INET and Stage II physical-fitness, written-examination, and recruitment-medical components; the final list is subject to the required qualifying stages and merit.

The IAF should be read separately through the applicable Agniveervayu intake notice. The Government of Odisha posting confirms a distinct Intake 02/2027 registration notice, illustrating why an applicant must not assume that Army CEE or Navy INET rules apply to IAF recruitment.
bullets: Expect both academic testing and physical/medical selection; clearing a written or online test alone does not establish final selection.
Download and follow the service-issued admit card for the precise reporting time, venue, identity-document, and original-certificate instructions.

sections.heading: Application process
paragraphs: Start on the official service portal, create or log into the candidate profile, select only categories for which the current notification confirms eligibility, complete the online form accurately, upload the required current photograph and documents, and pay a fee only through the official workflow where applicable. The Army’s 2026 notice says candidates must provide an active email address and mobile number, enter Aadhaar details, and use the Join Indian Army portal to check eligibility and apply.

Keep original educational, identity, domicile, caste/category, character, and other certificates specified by the admit card ready for later stages. A Ministry of Defence recruitment update for the Army specifically advises shortlisted candidates to monitor the official website, registered email, and mobile number for results and call letters, and to bring the requisite original documents to the rally.

Deadlines are volatile. As a dated illustration, the cited IAF Intake 02/2027 government posting lists June 23–July 26, 2026, while the cited Army notice used February 13–April 1, 2026. Always treat the live portal and current notice as authoritative.
bullets: Apply personally; recruitment is merit-based and official notices warn applicants not to use agents or pay bribes.
Save the application confirmation and payment record, and download the admit card only from the official portal.
Recheck the entered name, date of birth, education details, category, height, and uploaded photo before final submission; discrepancies can lead to cancellation.

sections.heading: Preparation strategy
paragraphs: Prepare in parallel for the online/written component and for physical and medical screening. For Army CEE, begin with the sample/practice material associated with the exact category: the cited notification says category-wise online CEE practice links and explanatory registration/exam videos are available through Join Indian Army. Study to the category’s notified pattern rather than a generic “Agniveer syllabus.”

Build fitness progressively and safely because the selection path can include a Physical Fitness Test, physical measurements, physical-efficiency events, and recruitment medical examination. Before reporting, reconcile every qualification and certificate with the wording of the current notice and admit card; keep originals and the required copies ready.

Use a fraud-aware approach. The Army notification and Ministry of Defence update both state that selection is fair, transparent, and merit-based and caution candidates against recruiters, intermediaries, or anyone claiming they can influence selection.
bullets: Create a timetable covering category-specific CEE/INET subjects, timed objective-question practice, and weak topics.
Practise running and prescribed fitness activities only after checking the current service/category standard and, where appropriate, seeking medical advice.
Conduct a document audit early: marksheets, identity documents, Aadhaar, domicile, certificates for any claimed category/relaxation, photographs, and other notification-specific forms.
Track only official portal notices, dashboard messages, registered email, and SMS updates.

sections.heading: Service outcome, pay context, and next steps
paragraphs: Selection leads to enrolment as an Agniveer for a four-year engagement and military training according to service requirements. It is not an automatic permanent appointment. The cited 2026 Army notice states that, after four years, Agniveers may be offered the opportunity to apply for permanent enrolment; selection is centralized and performance-based, and up to 25% of a specific batch may be enrolled in the regular cadre. The Navy SSR notice describes the same maximum-up-to-25% framework for its regular sailor cadre.

Pay and terminal-benefit figures should be read as dated policy/notice context. The cited 2026 Army notice lists a customised monthly package progressing from ₹30,000 in year one to ₹40,000 in year four, with 70% shown as in-hand pay and 30% contributed to the corpus, matched by the Government. It lists an approximate ₹10.04 lakh Seva Nidhi package after four years, excluding interest, and says there is no gratuity or pensionary benefit for the engagement period.

The cited Army notice also states that an Agniveer receives a detailed skill-set certificate at the end of engagement and is not eligible for ex-servicemen status. Candidates should plan for both possible pathways: performance-based consideration for regular enrolment and a civilian education, skill, or employment transition after the four-year term.
bullets: Do not treat retention as assured: it depends on service requirements, policy, eligibility, and performance.
Treat all salary, allowance, insurance, compensation, and Seva Nidhi figures as subject to the applicable current service notice and policy.
Retain official training, skill, and service records for later education or employment applications.
